The Palais de la Cité began its transformation from royal residence to the primary seat of judicial and parliamentary authority in the early 14th century under Philip IV (r. 1285–1314), who centralized royal justice by establishing regular sessions of the curia regis as the Parlement de Paris. title: Parlement of Paris
highlights: Lit de justice held by Louis XV of France at the Parlement of Paris in 1715 The Parlement of Paris (French: Parlement de Paris) was the oldest parlement in the Kingdom of France, formed in the 14th century.
